description: A parser that stops at the first syntax error instead of recovering, so users see only one error per compile and waste iterations.
body: Failure mode: the parser throws or returns on the first unexpected token with no resynchronization, so a file with several independent syntax errors reports only the first; the user fixes it, recompiles, and discovers the next, repeating slowly. Detection signals: only one diagnostic ever appears regardless of how many errors exist; the parser exits the moment it sees an unexpected token. Do not abort parsing on the first error. Safer fallback: add panic-mode or phrase-level recovery that skips to a synchronizing token and continues, suppress cascaded errors from an already-reported failure, and test on a corpus of multi-error programs expecting multiple accurate diagnostics.
